---
title: "María Nanton"
about:
  template: trestles
  image: images/perfil.jpeg
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/mcnanton
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/mar%C3%ADa-cristina-n-920170126/
    - text: "{{< ai orcid >}} ORCID"
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0003-0426-2879
    - icon: twitter
      text: twitter
      href: https://twitter.com/bynans1/
---

Hi! I’m María, a Data Scientist based in Argentina. I work at the Ministry of Health of the City of Buenos Aires, where I coordinate data science projects focused on public health monitoring, analysis, and decision-making. I also work at The Global Health Network, developing open-source and AI-based tools to support global health researchn.

I have over five years of experience building end-to-end data science solutions in public health, including data pipelines, analytical workflows, and machine learning–enabled systems applied in real-world health system and policy contexts. My work emphasizes reproducibility, software quality, interoperability, and the practical deployment of data products in public sector and research environments.

I am actively involved in open science and data communities. As an organizer of R-Ladies Buenos Aires since 2019 and a member of the R-Ladies Global Team, I work to promote diversity and inclusion in tech while advocating for open-source tools, collaborative development, and rigorous technical standards in data science.
